_readme: FRAME OVERRIDES — local control over any element in any frame.
This is the LAST layer applied. Precedence, lowest to highest:
1. public/animation/animation.json   the Animation Guider's spec (from/to, easing, idle, orbit)
2. public/animation/audio-cues.json  narration cues — overrides `start` only
3. THIS FILE                         overrides anything, and always wins
It exists so a frame can be corrected HERE, in minutes, without waiting for a
Claude Design re-export — and, critically, so those corrections SURVIVE the next
export. The SVGs are overwritten wholesale each round; this file is not.
Keys are the SVG basename, then the element id exactly as it appears in the SVG.
Run `node scripts/audit-overrides.js` to verify every id here still exists — a
typo or a renamed element is otherwise silent, which is the failure mode that has
bitten this project on every export so far.
PER-ELEMENT FIELDS (all optional — set only what you want to change):
at      number   seconds from SCENE start when the element enters (overrides cue+spec)
dur     number   ms the entrance takes
x, y    number   px offset applied on top of the element's own placement
scale   number   multiplier, 1 = unchanged
rotate  number   degrees
opacity number   final opacity, 0-1
hide    boolean  true removes the element entirely (artwork you do not want)
idle    object   { amp, period, rot } gentle loop after it settles, or false to stop it
orbit   object   { period, deg } continuous rotation, or false to stop it
PER-FRAME FIELDS (under "_frame"):
x, y    number   nudge the WHOLE frame's contents
scale   number   scale the whole frame about its centre
EXAMPLE — everything below is commented out by being under _example;
copy the shape, not the key:
"frame_9.svg": {
"icon_ship": { "scale": 0.55, "y": 8 },
"table_strategy": { "at": 2.1 },
"spot_helm": { "orbit": { "period": 26000, "deg": 360 } }
